channel.title: Bizerba SE & Co. KG — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Bizerba SE & Co. KG (bizerba).

image.title: Bizerba SE & Co. KG — recent CVEs

item.title: CVE-2025-12509 · 8.4 HIGH — Scripts for the module Global_Shipping executable on BRAIN2 Server
item.description: On a client with an admin user, a Global_Shipping script can be implemented. The script could later be executed on the BRAIN2 server with administrator rights.

item.title: CVE-2025-12508 · 8.4 HIGH — Unencrypted communication to Active Directory services
item.description: When using domain users as BRAIN2 users, communication with Active Directory services is unencrypted. This can lead to the interception of authentication data and compromise confidentiality.

item.title: CVE-2025-12507 · 8.8 HIGH — Insecure service configuration – unquoted path
item.description: The service Bizerba Communication Server (BCS) has an unquoted service path. Due to the way Windows searches the executable for the BCS service, malicious programs can be executed.

item.title: CVE-2025-6512 · 10 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Scripts within reports executable on BRAIN2 Server
item.description: On a client with a non-admin user, a script can be integrated into a report. The reports could later be executed on the BRAIN2 server with administrator rights.

item.title: CVE-2025-6513 · 9.3 CRITICAL — BRAIN2 Configuration file for database access not sufficiently secured
item.description: Standard Windows users can access the configuration file for database access of the BRAIN2 application and decrypt it.

item.title: CVE-2025-2820 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Denial of Service
item.description: An authenticated attacker can compromise the availability of the device via the network

item.title: CVE-2025-2819 · 6.6 MEDIUM — Unrestricted Fileupload
item.description: There is a risk of unauthorized file uploads in GT-SoftControl and potential file overwrites due to insufficient validation in the file selection process. This could lead to data integrity issues and unauthorized access by an authenticated privileged…
